name: 约翰内斯·勃拉姆斯
brief: 勃拉姆斯出生于汉堡一个贫寒的音乐家庭，少年时期曾在港口区的酒馆弹琴贴补家用。1853年，20岁的勃拉姆斯经小提琴家约阿希姆引荐拜访了舒曼夫妇，舒曼在《新音乐杂志》上发表了著名的《新道路》一文，将勃拉姆斯推上历史舞台。舒曼去世后，勃拉姆斯与克拉拉·舒曼保持了终身的深厚友谊（其间是否有爱情至今成谜）。他终身未婚，晚年定居维也纳，是当时最受尊崇的在世作曲家。
briefEn: Brahms was born into a poor musical family in Hamburg and as a youth played piano in waterfront taverns to help support the household. In 1853, at the age of twenty, he was introduced to the Schumanns by the violinist Joseph Joachim; Robert Schumann's famous essay Neue Bahnen ('New Paths') in the Neue Zeitschrift fur Musik launched Brahms onto the historical stage. After Schumann's death, Brahms maintained a lifelong and profound friendship with Clara Schumann — whether it ever became a love affair remains one of music's great mysteries. He never married, settled in Vienna in his later years, and was the most revered living composer of his time.
contribution: 勃拉姆斯在「新德意志乐派」（瓦格纳、李斯特）与古典传统之间坚持了自己的道路——他证明了在不放弃传统形式的前提下，仍然可以写出充满浪漫主义情感的伟大音乐。他花了21年才完成《第一交响曲》，被指挥家汉斯·冯·彪罗称为「贝多芬的第十交响曲」。他将贝多芬的主题发展技法与舒伯特式的旋律美融为一体。
contributionEn: Brahms charted his own course between the New German School of Wagner and Liszt and the Classical tradition, proving that great music brimming with Romantic feeling could still be written without abandoning traditional forms. He spent twenty-one years completing his First Symphony, which the conductor Hans von Bulow hailed as 'Beethoven's Tenth.' He seamlessly united Beethoven's developmental rigor with Schubertian melodic beauty.
style: 勃拉姆斯的音乐以结构的严密性和情感的内敛深沉著称。他的旋律悠长而充满秋意般的温暖；和声丰厚浓郁，大量使用三度和六度叠加的饱满音响。他是「展开性变奏」（Developing Variation）的大师——一个简单的动机通过不断的细微变形贯穿整部作品。他的音乐从不外露，激情总是克制在严谨的形式之中，如同深秋的熊熊暗火。
styleEn: Brahms's music is celebrated for its structural rigor and its deeply reserved emotional intensity. His melodies are long-breathed and suffused with an autumnal warmth; his harmony is rich and dense, making lavish use of thirds and sixths for a full-bodied sonority. He was the master of developing variation — a simple motif is subtly transformed and threaded through an entire work. His music never wears its heart on its sleeve; passion is always held in check within disciplined formal architecture, like a deep, glowing fire beneath the surface of late autumn.

famousScores.name: 《德意志安魂曲》 Op.45
famousScores.desc: 以德语圣经为词的安魂曲，非天主教仪式，而是对人类的普遍安慰
famousScores.descEn: A requiem set to German biblical texts — not a Catholic liturgy but a universal consolation for all humanity
famousScores.detail:
《德意志安魂曲》创作于1865至1868年间，其动因一直是音乐史上的讨论热点。勃拉姆斯于1856年目睹了精神崩溃的舒曼在精神病院离世，1865年又痛失挚爱的母亲——正是这双重的丧失促成了这部安魂曲的诞生。与传统拉丁文安魂曲不同，勃拉姆斯亲自从路德宗德语《圣经》中选取篇章，将重心由为死者祈求安息转移到对生者的安慰，体现了他深沉而不依附于教条的精神信仰。

作品首演颇具戏剧色彩。1867年12月在维也纳举行的前三乐章演出遭遇事故：鼓手误解了乐谱中的记号，在最安静的段落突然奏出了震耳欲聋的鼓声，当场引发哄笑，演出几乎被毁。真正的成功到来于1868年4月10日不莱梅大教堂的首演，那一夜令在场数百名听众潸然泪下。勃拉姆斯的父亲恰在几个月前去世，勃拉姆斯在演出后独自守坐在空旷的大教堂中。

在音乐史上，《德意志安魂曲》标志着浪漫主义宗教音乐的高峰，也是勃拉姆斯确立自身在维也纳乐坛首席地位的奠基之作。其宏大的合唱写作直接继承了亨德尔与巴赫的传统，而在和声与配器上又完全属于浪漫主义时代。此曲与莫扎特、威尔第的安魂曲并列，成为合唱史上最常被演出的三大安魂曲之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
A German Requiem was composed between 1865 and 1868, and its origins have long fascinated music historians. Brahms witnessed the final decline of Robert Schumann — who died in an asylum in 1856 — and then lost his beloved mother in 1865; these twin bereavements gave birth to the work. Unlike traditional Latin requiems, Brahms himself selected the texts from the Luther Bible, shifting the emphasis from intercession for the dead to consolation for the living, reflecting his profound but undogmatic personal faith.

The premiere unfolded dramatically. A performance of the first three movements in Vienna in December 1867 was nearly wrecked when a timpanist misread the score and crashed his drums into the most delicate passage, provoking laughter. The true triumph came on April 10, 1868, at Bremen Cathedral, where the complete work moved hundreds to tears. Brahms's father had died just months earlier; after the performance, Brahms sat alone in the empty cathedral for a long time.

In music history, A German Requiem marks the apex of Romantic sacred choral music and the work that established Brahms as the reigning figure of Viennese musical life. Its majestic choral writing draws directly on Handel and Bach, while its harmonic language and orchestration are wholly of the Romantic era. It stands alongside the requiems of Mozart and Verdi as one of the three most frequently performed choral masterpieces in the entire repertoire.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Brahms German Requiem Otto Klemperer Philharmonia

famousScores.name: 《第一交响曲》c小调 Op.68
famousScores.nameEn: Symphony No. 1 in C minor, Op. 68
famousScores.desc: 耗时21年的里程碑，被誉为「贝多芬的第十」
famousScores.descEn: A milestone twenty-one years in the making, hailed as 'Beethoven's Tenth'
famousScores.detail:
勃拉姆斯于1855年前后开始构思第一交响曲，彼时他年仅22岁；然而直到1876年，43岁的他才在卡尔斯鲁厄完成首演——中间整整隔了21年。这段漫长的沉默并非懒惰，而是极度的自我要求：贝多芬九部交响曲的巨大阴影始终压在他头上，勃拉姆斯曾对友人说「你无法想象，当你身后不断传来那个巨人的脚步声时，作曲是多么艰难」。

终乐章中著名的「阿尔卑斯主题」据说源自勃拉姆斯1868年在克拉拉·舒曼生日时写下的一段圆号旋律，他在乐谱上附上了诗句：「高山上，深谷中，我向你致以千声祝贺！」这条旋律与贝多芬《第九》「欢乐颂」的主题有着惊人的相似，首演当晚有观众大声喊出「贝多芬！」勃拉姆斯淡然回答：「任何蠢蛋都能看出这一点。」

汉斯·冯·彪罗在首演后将其誉为「贝多芬的第十交响曲」，这个称号既是最高赞誉，也成为压在勃拉姆斯身上一生的标签。作品在音乐史上的意义在于：它证明了在瓦格纳乐剧和李斯特交响诗盛行的时代，绝对音乐的交响曲传统依然具有无限的生命力，为后来的布鲁克纳、马勒乃至20世纪的交响作曲家照亮了道路。
famousScores.detailEn:
Brahms began sketching the First Symphony around 1855, when he was barely twenty-two; yet the premiere did not take place until 1876 in Karlsruhe, when he was forty-three — a gap of twenty-one years. This long silence was not idleness but extreme self-demand: the towering shadow of Beethoven's nine symphonies never left him, and he once told a friend, 'You have no idea how it feels to hear the footsteps of that giant behind you.'

The famous 'Alpine theme' in the finale is said to derive from a horn melody Brahms wrote for Clara Schumann's birthday in 1868, accompanied by the words: 'High on the mountain, deep in the valley, I send you a thousand greetings!' This melody bears a striking resemblance to the 'Ode to Joy' from Beethoven's Ninth, and at the premiere an audience member reportedly called out 'Beethoven!' Brahms replied drily: 'Any fool can see that.'

Hans von Bülow hailed it after the premiere as 'Beethoven's Tenth' — the highest compliment, yet also a label Brahms carried for the rest of his life. The symphony's historical significance lies in demonstrating that in an era dominated by Wagnerian music drama and Lisztian symphonic poems, the tradition of absolute instrumental music still had boundless vitality — lighting the way for Bruckner, Mahler, and twentieth-century symphonists.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Brahms Symphony 1 Carlos Kleiber Vienna Philharmonic

famousScores.name: 《第四交响曲》e小调 Op.98
famousScores.nameEn: Symphony No. 4 in E minor, Op. 98
famousScores.desc: 末乐章以帕萨卡利亚形式写就，古典形式的浪漫主义巅峰
famousScores.descEn: Its finale is cast as a passacaglia — the crowning achievement of Romantic expression within Classical form
famousScores.detail:
《第四交响曲》完成于1885年，是勃拉姆斯的最后一部交响曲，也被普遍认为是他最深刻的交响作品。末乐章是一首严格的帕萨卡利亚（古老的变奏形式），建立在一个八音节的低音固定音型之上，共有30个变奏。勃拉姆斯刻意从巴赫《b小调弥撒》中借用了同样的低音主题，在19世纪写出了一首17世纪的音乐形式——这在当时是惊人之举。

首演前，勃拉姆斯将四手联弹版本弹给朋友汉斯·冯·彪罗试听。彪罗听后沉默良久，然后说：「我现在想知道，一个人究竟能够想出什么来接在这之后？」这句话道出了《第四》的终结性——这部交响曲是对整个德奥交响传统的一次庄严告别。指挥家卡尔·波姆曾说，每次指挥这部作品的末乐章，都感觉是在陪伴一个伟大的灵魂步入黑暗。

从音乐史的角度看，《第四交响曲》既是浪漫主义交响曲的巅峰，也是向巴洛克对位传统的致敬。勃拉姆斯将最古老的形式（帕萨卡利亚）灌注了最浓烈的浪漫主义情感，证明形式与内容之间的张力本身就是最强大的艺术动力。勋伯格后来将这种将古老形式赋予新内容的做法称为「进步的勃拉姆斯」。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Fourth Symphony, completed in 1885, is Brahms's final symphony and widely regarded as his most profound. The finale is a strict passacaglia — an ancient variation form built on a repeating eight-note bass line — with thirty variations. Brahms deliberately borrowed the same bass theme from Bach's Mass in B minor, effectively writing a seventeenth-century form in the nineteenth century, a breathtaking act of historical reach.

Before the premiere, Brahms played a four-hand arrangement for his friend Hans von Bülow. After a long silence, Bülow said: 'I want to know what one can possibly think of to come after this.' The remark captures the symphony's quality of finality — it feels like a solemn farewell to the entire Austro-German symphonic tradition. Conductor Karl Böhm once said that conducting the finale always felt like accompanying a great spirit into darkness.

Historically, the Fourth Symphony is simultaneously the apex of Romantic symphonic writing and a homage to Baroque contrapuntal tradition. Brahms poured the most intense Romantic emotion into the most ancient of forms — the passacaglia — proving that the tension between form and content is itself the most powerful artistic force. Schoenberg would later cite this very quality when he coined the phrase 'Brahms the Progressive.'
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Brahms Symphony 4 Carlos Kleiber Vienna Philharmonic 1980

famousScores.name: 《小提琴协奏曲》D大调 Op.77
famousScores.nameEn: Violin Concerto in D major, Op. 77
famousScores.desc: 与贝多芬、柴可夫斯基并称三大小提琴协奏曲
famousScores.descEn: Ranked alongside Beethoven's and Tchaikovsky's as one of the three great violin concertos
famousScores.detail:
《小提琴协奏曲》创作于1878年，是勃拉姆斯与好友、小提琴大师约瑟夫·约阿希姆深度合作的产物。勃拉姆斯将草稿寄给约阿希姆，请他就演奏技巧和可行性提出意见，两人往来书信多达数十封，约阿希姆建议的多处技术修改都被勃拉姆斯采纳。这段创作过程本身就是音乐家之间珍贵友谊的见证。

作品首演后，著名小提琴家汉斯·冯·彪罗开玩笑称之为「反小提琴协奏曲」——因为交响乐队的分量极重，小提琴几乎要「对抗」庞大的管弦乐而存在，与当时那些旋律华美、炫技优先的协奏曲风格迥异。据说当时还有人批评说这是「为小提琴所作，反对小提琴的协奏曲」。这种特性反而成为它独特的魅力所在：独奏与乐队是平等的对话者，而非前者压过后者。

从音乐史的角度看，《小提琴协奏曲》与贝多芬、柴可夫斯基的小提琴协奏曲并列为该体裁的三座高峰。慢乐章中双簧管吹出的如泣如诉的旋律，是整个浪漫主义时代最令人心碎的段落之一。此曲的问世直接影响了埃尔加、西贝柳斯和巴托克的协奏曲写作观念。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Violin Concerto was composed in 1878 as a profound collaboration between Brahms and his close friend, the great violinist Joseph Joachim. Brahms sent his sketches to Joachim and invited his advice on technique and playability; dozens of letters were exchanged, and many of Joachim's suggested technical revisions were incorporated. The creative process itself stands as a testament to a rare friendship between musicians.

After the premiere, Hans von Bülow jokingly called it an 'anti-violin concerto,' because the orchestral weight is so immense that the violin seems to battle against the full forces of the ensemble — utterly unlike the melodically lavish, virtuosity-first concertos then in fashion. One contemporary critic quipped that it was 'a concerto for violin against orchestra.' This very quality became its defining appeal: soloist and orchestra are equal partners in dialogue.

Historically, the Violin Concerto stands alongside those of Beethoven and Tchaikovsky as one of the three supreme achievements in the genre. The heartbreaking oboe melody in the slow movement is one of the most affecting moments in all Romantic music. Its appearance directly influenced the concerto aesthetics of Elgar, Sibelius, and Bartók.

famousScores.name: 《间奏曲》Op.117–119
famousScores.desc: 晚年钢琴小品，秋日余晖般的深沉与温柔
famousScores.descEn: Late piano miniatures of autumnal depth and tenderness, like the last glow of a setting sun
famousScores.detail:
Op.117至Op.119这三套钢琴小品创作于1892至1893年，是勃拉姆斯最后的钢琴独奏作品，也是他内心世界最私密的自白。勃拉姆斯将Op.117的第一首《间奏曲》称为「我痛苦的摇篮曲」，并在乐谱上题写了苏格兰古谣「沉睡吧，沉睡吧，宝贝，入眠，你的哭泣让我心伤」。这些作品没有任何炫技，只有极度浓缩的情感与沉思。

这些小品是在克拉拉·舒曼日益衰老（她于1896年去世）的背景下写成的。许多学者认为，这些作品是勃拉姆斯对整个浪漫主义时代的告别——对他一生的悲欢离合、对克拉拉、对年轻时的激情，以及对即将逝去的一切发出的轻柔叹息。勃拉姆斯本人选择以这些小品结束他的钢琴创作生涯，意味深长。

在音乐史上，这些晚期小品被视为浪漫主义钢琴音乐从宏大叙事转向内省独白的重要转折点。它们影响了福雷晚期的夜曲和即兴曲，以及勋伯格早期的表现主义钢琴作品。钢琴家拉度·鲁普、毕希纳都认为，演奏这些小品需要「化繁为简」的最高境界——用最少的音符说出最多的话。
famousScores.detailEn:
The three sets of piano pieces comprising Op. 117 to Op. 119 were composed in 1892–93, representing Brahms's final piano works and the most intimate confessions of his inner life. Brahms himself described the first Intermezzo of Op. 117 as 'the lullaby of all my griefs,' and inscribed on the score a line from a Scottish folk song: 'Sleep softly, my child, sleep softly and well; it pains me to see you weep.' These pieces contain no virtuosity — only emotion refined to its purest essence.

They were written as Clara Schumann was fading (she died in 1896). Many scholars see these works as Brahms's farewell to the entire Romantic era — a tender sigh directed at the joys and sorrows of his whole life, at Clara, at the passions of his youth, and at everything about to pass away. That Brahms chose these miniatures to close his career as a piano composer is itself deeply eloquent.

In music history, these late pieces mark a crucial turning point from the grand narratives of Romantic piano music toward intimate introspection. They influenced Fauré's late nocturnes and impromptus and Schoenberg's early expressionist piano works. Pianists from Radu Lupu to Alfred Brendel have argued that performing them demands the highest mastery of 'less as more' — saying the most with the fewest notes.

famousScores.name: 《学术节庆序曲》 Op.80
famousScores.desc: 以德国学生饮酒歌为素材，感谢布雷斯劳大学授予名誉博士的幽默致谢曲
famousScores.descEn: A humorous tribute woven from German student drinking songs, written in thanks for an honorary doctorate from Breslau University
famousScores.detail:
1879年，布雷斯劳大学授予勃拉姆斯名誉博士学位。按惯例，受奖者应以某种正式作品答谢，勃拉姆斯却别出心裁，写出了这首将多首德国大学学生歌曲（包括著名的饮酒歌《我们建造了一座宏伟的宫殿》和《嗬，学子们，赶快活跃起来》）编织成一部热闹辉煌的管弦乐曲。勃拉姆斯私下承认，他觉得必须写一首「名副其实的学术作品」，结果却交出了一首充满狂欢气息的狂想曲。

这其中有一段著名的逸事：按照惯例，布雷斯劳大学授予名誉博士学位时有一套庄重的拉丁语仪式，学校期待收到一部庄严的清唱剧或管弦乐曲作为答谢。当这首以饮酒歌为素材的序曲在1881年首演后，校方官员一时不知如何反应——这究竟是嘲讽还是致敬？而听众的热烈掌声给出了最终答案。勃拉姆斯以这种轻松的幽默证明了自己并非只会写沉重的作品。

《学术节庆序曲》与同时创作的《悲剧序曲》Op.81形成了鲜明对比：前者是节日的笑声，后者是肃穆的哀歌。两首作品在同年首演，如同一枚硬币的两面，完美揭示了勃拉姆斯音乐气质中的双重性——严肃与幽默、悲剧与欢乐并存。此曲影响了理查·施特劳斯《家庭交响曲》等以通俗素材入乐的创作手法。
famousScores.detailEn:
In 1879, Breslau University conferred an honorary doctorate on Brahms. Convention called for a formal piece of thanks; Brahms responded with a work that wove together several well-known German student songs — including the drinking song 'Wir hatten gebauet ein stattliches Haus' and 'Gaudeamus igitur' — into a boisterous, brilliantly orchestrated overture. Brahms privately acknowledged that he felt obliged to produce something in 'proper academic form,' yet what he delivered was a joyous carnival piece.

A famous story surrounds the premiere: the university had expected a solemn cantata or ceremonial overture in reply to their honor. When this student-song fantasia premiered in 1881, officials were momentarily unsure whether it was satire or tribute. The audience's thunderous applause settled the question. Brahms proved with this light-hearted work that he was far more than a purveyor of weighty musical gravity.

The Academic Festival Overture stands in pointed contrast to its companion piece, the Tragic Overture Op. 81, composed at the same time: one is the laughter of a festival, the other a solemn elegy. Premiered together in the same season, they form two sides of a single coin, revealing the twin poles of Brahms's personality — gravity and wit, tragedy and celebration — held in perfect balance. The work influenced later composers, including Richard Strauss, in the technique of elevating popular and communal material into serious orchestral art.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Brahms Academic Festival Overture Abbado Berlin

famousScores.name: 《小提琴与大提琴双重协奏曲》a小调 Op.102
famousScores.nameEn: Double Concerto for Violin and Cello in A minor, Op. 102
famousScores.desc: 勃拉姆斯最后的管弦乐作品，为修复与约阿希姆的友谊而作
famousScores.descEn: Brahms's final orchestral work, composed in part as a gesture of reconciliation with his friend Joseph Joachim
famousScores.detail:
《双重协奏曲》完成于1887年，是勃拉姆斯最后一部管弦乐作品，其创作背后有一段动人的友谊故事。勃拉姆斯与小提琴家约阿希姆是长达三十余年的挚友，但1880年代初，约阿希姆与妻子阿米莉·维斯离婚，勃拉姆斯却出庭为阿米莉作证，认为她无辜受到约阿希姆猜疑，此事令约阿希姆与勃拉姆斯关系彻底破裂。这部双重协奏曲正是勃拉姆斯向约阿希姆伸出的和解之手——以音乐替代无法说出口的道歉。

勃拉姆斯将独奏的小提琴声部写给约阿希姆，大提琴声部则写给约阿希姆的亲密合作伙伴罗伯特·豪斯曼。约阿希姆接受了这份邀约，两人于1887年10月18日在科隆携手完成了首演，沉默多年的友谊就此重续。克拉拉·舒曼在信中写道：「这两个男人就这样用音乐和好了，我的心里说不出的感动。」

从音乐史的角度看，双重协奏曲是一个极为罕见且古老的体裁——勃拉姆斯是在有意向巴赫的双小提琴协奏曲致敬。作品在技术上极为艰深，两件独奏乐器与乐队之间的对话既平衡又充满张力。尽管作品并不如《小提琴协奏曲》那样耳熟能详，但许多音乐家认为其深沉的内敛气质使它成为勃拉姆斯最有深度的协奏曲作品。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Double Concerto was completed in 1887 as Brahms's final orchestral work, and behind its creation lies a moving story of friendship. Brahms and the violinist Joseph Joachim had been intimate friends for over thirty years, but in the early 1880s their relationship broke down completely when Joachim divorced his wife Amalie Weiss and Brahms testified on her behalf, declaring her innocent of Joachim's suspicions. The Double Concerto was Brahms's outstretched hand of reconciliation — an apology expressed in music rather than words.

Brahms wrote the violin part for Joachim and the cello part for Joachim's close collaborator Robert Hausmann. Joachim accepted the invitation, and on October 18, 1887, in Cologne, the two men performed the premiere together, mending a long silence. Clara Schumann wrote in a letter: 'These two men made up with each other through music; I cannot find words for how deeply this moved me.'

Historically, the double concerto is an exceptionally rare and ancient form — Brahms was consciously paying homage to Bach's Double Violin Concerto. The work is technically formidable, with the two soloists and the orchestra engaged in a dialogue that is both perfectly balanced and full of tension. Though less immediately familiar than the Violin Concerto, many musicians consider its deeply inward character to make it Brahms's most profound concerto.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Brahms Double Concerto Oistrakh Rostropovich Szell

famousScores.name: 《第二钢琴协奏曲》降B大调 Op.83
famousScores.nameEn: Piano Concerto No. 2 in B-flat major, Op. 83
famousScores.desc: 四乐章巨构，堪称「钢琴与乐队的交响曲」，技术与思想的双重巅峰
famousScores.descEn: A four-movement colossus described as a 'symphony with piano,' the pinnacle of both technical and intellectual ambition
famousScores.detail:
《第二钢琴协奏曲》完成于1881年，距第一钢琴协奏曲（1858年）已过去了整整二十二年。这部作品是勃拉姆斯最宏大的钢琴作品，以四个乐章（加入了谐谑曲）的巨大体量打破了协奏曲的常规框架。汉斯·冯·彪罗将其称为「带有钢琴独奏的交响曲」，因为钢琴从头到尾作为乐队的平等成员参与音乐的发展，而非凌驾其上的炫技表演者。

勃拉姆斯本人是这首协奏曲的首演独奏者（1881年11月9日，布达佩斯）。演出结束后，有观众好奇地问他在演出期间感受如何，勃拉姆斯一贯的直率给出了令人忍俊不禁的回答：「我听到的第三乐章的大提琴独奏太美了。」（言下之意是他自己弹得相当不理想。）他随后在欧洲各地巡演演奏这部作品达数年之久。

在钢琴协奏曲历史上，《第二钢琴协奏曲》是最后一批「反炫技」协奏曲的高峰之一——它将深刻的交响性思维置于技巧展示之上。慢乐章中的大提琴独奏是整部西方器乐文献中最令人心碎的段落之一。这部作品奠定了现代钢琴协奏曲创作中「思想重于技巧」原则，深远影响了拉赫玛尼诺夫《第二钢琴协奏曲》的创作理念。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Second Piano Concerto was completed in 1881, twenty-two years after the First. It is Brahms's grandest piano work, its four movements (including a scherzo) shattering the conventional concerto framework. Hans von Bülow famously called it 'a symphony with piano obbligato,' because the piano participates throughout as an equal member of the ensemble rather than as a virtuoso showpiece commanding the stage.

Brahms himself was the soloist at the premiere in Budapest on November 9, 1881. Afterward, an audience member asked how he felt during the performance. Brahms replied with characteristic bluntness: 'The cello solo in the third movement sounded very beautiful.' The clear implication was that he himself had played rather poorly. He nonetheless went on to tour the work across Europe for several years.

In the history of the piano concerto, the Second Concerto stands as one of the final great anti-virtuosic statements — placing symphonic depth above technical display. The cello solo in the slow movement is among the most heartbreaking passages in all Western instrumental music. The work profoundly shaped the aesthetic of later composers, including Rachmaninoff, in establishing the principle that intellectual substance must take precedence over pianism.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Brahms Piano Concerto 2 Emil Gilels Jochum

famousScores.name: 《单簧管五重奏》b小调 Op.115
famousScores.nameEn: Clarinet Quintet in B minor, Op. 115
famousScores.desc: 为单簧管与弦乐四重奏而作，晚秋夕照般的绝世之作
famousScores.descEn: For clarinet and string quartet — a work of incomparable autumnal beauty, among the finest chamber pieces ever written
famousScores.detail:
1891年，勃拉姆斯在梅宁根宫廷乐团邂逅了单簧管演奏家理查德·米尔菲尔德，后者的演奏技艺令他如遭雷击。勃拉姆斯曾宣布退出创作，正是米尔菲尔德的演奏打破了他的沉默：在随后的几年里，他为米尔菲尔德量身创作了两首单簧管奏鸣曲和两首单簧管室内乐，其中《单簧管五重奏》Op.115被公认为其中最伟大的一部。

这部五重奏首演于1891年12月12日柏林，由米尔菲尔德担任单簧管独奏，约阿希姆弦乐四重奏担任弦乐声部。首演之后，评论界立刻意识到这是一部划时代的杰作。作品末乐章以对第一乐章主题的遥远回忆作结——不是激烈的终结，而是一种悄然消逝，如同烛光的缓缓熄灭，让人想起叶芝的诗句「万物皆归为寂灭」。

《单簧管五重奏》与莫扎特K.581同为单簧管室内乐文献的双峰并峙。从音乐史角度看，它是浪漫主义晚期室内乐逐渐走向内省与简洁的重要先兆：不再追求宏大的戏剧效果，而是在最亲密的音响空间里寻求最深刻的情感共鸣。它对弗兰克、雷格乃至20世纪初室内乐写作的影响至今仍被学界广泛讨论。
famousScores.detailEn:
In 1891, Brahms encountered the clarinettist Richard Mühlfeld at the Meiningen court orchestra, and Mühlfeld's playing struck him like lightning. Brahms had already announced his retirement from composition, but Mühlfeld's artistry shattered his silence: over the following years he wrote two clarinet sonatas and two chamber works featuring the instrument expressly for Mühlfeld, of which the Clarinet Quintet Op. 115 is universally regarded as the greatest.

The work had its premiere on December 12, 1891, in Berlin, with Mühlfeld as soloist and the Joachim String Quartet. Critics immediately recognized it as a masterpiece. The finale closes with a distant, ghostly echo of the opening movement's theme — not a forceful conclusion but a quiet disappearance, like a candle slowly extinguishing, calling to mind Yeats's 'all things fall and are built again.'

The Clarinet Quintet stands alongside Mozart's K. 581 as one of the two supreme peaks of the clarinet chamber music repertoire. In music history it marks an important turn in late Romantic chamber music toward introspection and economy — away from grand dramatic gestures, toward the deepest emotional resonance within the most intimate acoustic space. Its influence on Franck, Reger, and early twentieth-century chamber writing continues to be widely discussed.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Brahms Clarinet Quintet Karl Leister Amadeus Quartet

famousScores.name: 《第二交响曲》D大调 Op.73
famousScores.nameEn: Symphony No. 2 in D major, Op. 73
famousScores.desc: 被称为「勃拉姆斯的田园」，明朗温暖，是他四部交响曲中最开朗的一首
famousScores.descEn: Called 'Brahms's Pastoral,' it is the most serene and warmly glowing of his four symphonies
famousScores.detail:
《第二交响曲》创作于1877年的夏天，彼时勃拉姆斯正在奥地利南部的佩尔查赫湖畔度假，那里阳光明媚、青山如黛。整部作品仅用了大约四个月便完成，速度令人惊讶——对比《第一交响曲》的漫长21年，这部作品简直像是从大自然中自然流淌出来的。勃拉姆斯在给出版商西姆洛克的信中打趣道：「这首新交响曲如此令人愉快，你只需要让它印在蓝色封面上，印几株白羊和几棵老橡树。」

然而勃拉姆斯在轻松的表面背后一如既往地藏着精心的设计。开场的三音动机（D-升C-D）贯穿全曲四个乐章，经历了无数次「展开性变奏」。终乐章尾声处，弦乐与铜管如洪水般涌来，将全曲推向令人振奋的高潮——这并非田园风光的平静收尾，而是经历了沉思与变奏后自然迸发的生命之力。

指挥家汉斯·里希特在听完《第二交响曲》后将其与贝多芬《第六「田园」交响曲》相提并论，此后「勃拉姆斯的田园」这一称号便广泛流传。在音乐史上，这部交响曲以其明朗的气质和高超的旋律天赋证明了勃拉姆斯绝非只是一位沉重的「悲秋者」，而是同样能写出充满生命喜悦的音乐。它也是交响音乐会中最受观众欢迎的勃拉姆斯交响曲之一。
famousScores.detailEn:
The Second Symphony was composed in the summer of 1877 while Brahms was vacationing at Pörtschach on the Wörthersee in southern Austria — sunshine, green hills, and clear water. The entire work was completed in roughly four months, a remarkable speed compared to the twenty-one-year gestation of the First. Brahms wrote to his publisher Simrock with a laugh: 'The new symphony is so cheerful you only need to have it printed with a blue cover, a few little white lambs, and some old oak trees.'

Yet beneath the sunny surface lay his customary craft: the opening three-note motif (D–C-sharp–D) permeates all four movements through countless acts of developing variation. The finale's coda surges with strings and brass in a great wave of exhilaration — not the tranquil close of a pastoral idyll, but the explosive vitality that naturally follows sustained contemplation and transformation.

Conductor Hans Richter compared the Second Symphony to Beethoven's 'Pastoral' Sixth after hearing it, and the nickname has stuck ever since. In music history this symphony demonstrates that Brahms was far more than a somber autumnal spirit — he was equally capable of music brimming with the joy of life. It remains one of the most beloved of all Brahms symphonies with concert audiences worldwide.
famousScores.spotifyQuery: Brahms Symphony 2 Carlos Kleiber Vienna Philharmonic

influence: 勃拉姆斯代表了浪漫主义时期「绝对音乐」（纯音乐）立场的最高成就。勋伯格在著名论文《进步的勃拉姆斯》中揭示了勃拉姆斯音乐中隐藏的现代性——他的「展开性变奏」技法是十二音技法的直接先驱。他对室内乐、艺术歌曲和交响曲的贡献影响了理查·施特劳斯、雷格、勋伯格和韦伯恩。他证明了传统与创新并不矛盾。
influenceEn: Brahms represents the supreme achievement of the 'absolute music' ideal in the Romantic era. In his landmark essay 'Brahms the Progressive,' Schoenberg revealed the hidden modernity in Brahms's music — his developing variation technique was a direct precursor to the twelve-tone method. His contributions to chamber music, art song, and the symphony influenced Richard Strauss, Reger, Schoenberg, and Webern. He proved that tradition and innovation are not mutually exclusive.
